why: This week's calibration run across 6 signal rows resulted in targeted upward adjustments to three agents: Agent 2 (INVEST Quality) received a +1 base confidence increase (65→66) on the back of a 2.2% FP rate well below the 5% threshold, Agent 44 (FCA Evidence Pack) similarly gained +1 (65→66) with a 3.3% FP rate, and Agent 33 (Test Coverage Analyser) received the largest uplift of +2 (65→67) after recording a perfect 0.0% FP rate, reflecting consistently high signal precision. Agents 1 (Story Intent), 3 (FCA Classifier), and 5 (AC Generator) were held at their current baselines — Agents 1 and 3 remain stable within acceptable bounds at 6.7% and 4.4% respectively, while Agent 5 (AC Generator) warrants monitoring attention as its 10.5% FP rate is the highest observed this week and, although still classified as within acceptable range, is approaching a level that may warrant a threshold review in the next calibration cycle.

data.calibration_summary: This week's calibration run across 6 signal rows resulted in targeted upward adjustments to three agents: Agent 2 (INVEST Quality) received a +1 base confidence increase (65→66) on the back of a 2.2% FP rate well below the 5% threshold, Agent 44 (FCA Evidence Pack) similarly gained +1 (65→66) with a 3.3% FP rate, and Agent 33 (Test Coverage Analyser) received the largest uplift of +2 (65→67) after recording a perfect 0.0% FP rate, reflecting consistently high signal precision. Agents 1 (Story Intent), 3 (FCA Classifier), and 5 (AC Generator) were held at their current baselines — Agents 1 and 3 remain stable within acceptable bounds at 6.7% and 4.4% respectively, while Agent 5 (AC Generator) warrants monitoring attention as its 10.5% FP rate is the highest observed this week and, although still classified as within acceptable range, is approaching a level that may warrant a threshold review in the next calibration cycle.
data.key_insight: Agent 5 (AC Generator) posted the week's highest false-positive rate at 10.5%, making it the primary candidate for close monitoring ahead of the next calibration run.
